chunk_summary: This section provides a day 0–3 enteral nutrition advancement template, including feeding initiation, progression targets, and triggers for supplemental parenteral nutrition, with specific guidelines for preterm and VLBW infants.
learning_objectives: Understand the stepwise approach to enteral feeding advancement during the first 3 days.
Identify criteria for initiating and advancing enteral nutrition in preterm and VLBW infants.
Recognize signs of feeding intolerance and appropriate interventions.
Learn when to consider supplemental parenteral nutrition based on enteral nutrition delivery.
key_facts: Day 0: Start full-strength polymeric formula at ~1 mL/kg/hour (continuous) or bolus feeds at ~25% of goal daily volume.
Day 1: Advance to ~50% of goal if stable; adjust feeding method if intolerance occurs.
Day 2: Progress to 75–100% of goal based on illness severity and nutrition risk.
Preterm/VLBW infants: Trophic feeds of ~10–15 mL/kg/day can start within 24 hours if stable, advancing to ~150–180 mL/kg/day over 1–2 weeks.
Day 3: If enteral nutrition remains <60–80% of needs, consider energy-dense formulas, postpyloric access, or supplemental parenteral nutrition.
reference_quote: In preterm infants, small green residuals alone (for example <2–3 mL) should not automatically slow advancement without other intolerance signs. (Source: https://iapindia.org/pdf/Ch-098-Feeding-in-Preterm-and-Feed-Intolerance.pdf)
